title: Translating Embeddings for Modeling Multi-relational Data
abstract: We propose a method that models entities and relations in knowledge graphs as translations in embedding space, learning vector representations that capture relational patterns.

title: Convolutional 2D Knowledge Graph Embeddings
abstract: We introduce ConvE, a convolutional approach to knowledge graph embedding that reshapes entity and relation embeddings into 2D grids and applies convolutional filters to capture interaction patterns.

title: RotatE: Knowledge Graph Embedding by Relational Rotation in Complex Space
abstract: We propose RotatE, which models relations as rotations in complex vector space, enabling the modeling of various relation patterns including symmetry, inversion, and composition.

title: Recurrent Event Network: Autoregressive Structure Inference over Temporal Knowledge Graphs
abstract: We present RE-NET, an autoregressive architecture for temporal knowledge graph reasoning that uses an RNN to evolve entity representations over time and a neighborhood aggregator to capture structural patterns.

title: Learning from History: Modeling Temporal Knowledge Graphs with Sequential Copy-Generation Network
abstract: We propose CyGNet, which combines copy and generation mechanisms to leverage historical fact repetition patterns for temporal knowledge graph completion.

title: Neural Turing Machines
abstract: We introduce Neural Turing Machines that couple neural networks with external memory matrices and differentiable addressing mechanisms for algorithmic reasoning tasks.

title: Hybrid Computing Using a Neural Network with Dynamic External Memory
abstract: We present the Differentiable Neural Computer, extending NTMs with dynamic memory allocation and temporal memory linking for complex reasoning.

title: End-To-End Memory Networks
abstract: We propose a recurrent neural network architecture with a memory component that can be read and written to for multi-hop reasoning over input sequences.

title: Attention Is All You Need
abstract: We propose the Transformer architecture based entirely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions, with sinusoidal positional encodings for sequence order.

title: Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization
abstract: We propose AdamW, which decouples weight decay from the gradient-based optimization step, improving regularization in adaptive gradient methods.
